questions.q: A company purchased a machine for ₹ 50,000. The payment was made by issuing a cheque. Pass the journal entry.
questions.answer: Dr. Machine A/c ₹ 50,000 Cr. Bank A/c ₹ 50,000
Cash discount is a discount allowed by the seller to the buyer for making payment in cash.

questions.q: A Ltd. paid ₹ 10,000 as salaries to its employees on 31.03.2023. Pass the journal entry.
questions.answer: Dr. Salary A/c ₹ 10,000 Cr. Bank A/c ₹ 10,000

questions.q: Which of the following is not a method of preparing the trial balance?
A Ltd. purchased goods worth ₹ 20,000 from B Ltd. on 31.12.2022. The mode of payment was by cheque. Pass the journal entry.
questions.answer: Dr. Purchases A/c ₹ 20,000 Cr. Bank A/c ₹ 20,000

questions.q: What is the difference between 'bad debts' and 'provision for bad and doubtful debts'?
questions.answer: Bad debts are the debts that are considered to be completely worthless, while provision for bad and doubtful debts is an estimate of the debts that may become bad in the future.

questions.q: A company received ₹ 10,000 from its customer as advance payment for next year's purchase. Pass the journal entry.
questions.answer: Dr. Cash A/c ₹ 10,000 Cr. Advance from Customer A/c ₹ 10,000

questions.q: Which of the following is a contra-entry?
A Ltd. paid ₹ 5,000 as rent for the year 2023 on 31.12.2022. Pass the journal entry.
questions.answer: Dr. Rent A/c ₹ 5,000 Cr. Bank A/c ₹ 5,000

questions.q: What is the purpose of preparing a 'trading and profit and loss account'?
questions.answer: The purpose of preparing a trading and profit and loss account is to calculate the net profit or net loss of the business.
